Title: A reference information systems architecture for collaborative condition monitoring
Title_1: A reference information systems architecture for collaborative condition monitoring
Authors: Philipp Hagenhoff, Anna Gieß, Frederik Möller, Boris Otto
Architecture summary: A decentralized industrial data space architecture integrating edge and cloud layers for collaborative condition monitoring. Sensor and machine data are processed locally and shared securely across factory operators, machine builders, and component suppliers through standardized connectors, dashboards, and governance mechanisms to support predictive maintenance and anomaly detection while preserving data sovereignty.

Title: Data-Sovereign Architecture for Quality-As-A-Service Across Industrial Supply Chain Partners
Title_1: Data-Sovereign Architecture for Quality-As-A-Service Across Industrial Supply Chain Partners
Authors: Jose Moises Araya-Martinez, Ajay Menon Kannanthodath Induchoodana, Srinidhi Krishna Kalpathy Sundaram, David Görzig, Johannes Diemer, Jens Lambrecht, Jörg Krüger
Responsibility: Provides files, requests computation, receives results.
Responsibility_3: Receives files, trains model, computes using sensor data, sends results.
Architecture summary: A federated industrial Quality-as-a-Service architecture where suppliers locally train and execute AI quality inspection models using synthetic CAD-derived data, standardize outputs via AAS/IDTA, and securely exchange quality metrics through EDC dataspaces without transferring large raw datasets.

Title: A Data Space infrastructure supporting the integration of clinical data nodes and cancer registries to improve personalized medicine
Title_1: A Data Space infrastructure supporting the integration of clinical data nodes and cancer registries to improve personalized medicine
Authors: Florim Hamiti, Martin Breidenbach, Naguib Heiba, Aynur Guluzade, Yehya Mohamad, Carlos A Velasco
Security measures: Legal agreements; Firewall; Authentication; Manual approval
Responsibility: Holds data, compute and protect said data.
Responsibility_3: Connects the different nodes, anonymizes data.
Responsibility_5: Connect to trust center to request computation result.
Architecture summary: Distributed EHDS-compatible healthcare data space where clinical nodes and cancer registries keep local control of data while secure queries and aggregated analyses are coordinated through a central data services/trust infrastructure using IDS connectors and security controls.

Title: PERSEUS: Protection-Enhanced Resilient System for Securing Ubiquitous Healthcare Solutions
Title_1: PERSEUS: Protection-Enhanced Resilient System for Securing Ubiquitous Healthcare Solutions
Authors: Miguel Landry Foko Sindjoung, Garrik Brel Jagho Mdemaya, Martinien Deffo Foko, Mthulisi Velempini
Data storage: Cloud; Edge device; Central server, database
Responsibility: Data collection, send to server.
Responsibility_3: Processing, primary and backup servers for security.
Responsibility_5: Orchestration, computation of hungry tasks.
Architecture summary: Secure IoMT healthcare architecture using MEC, SDN, IDS/IPS, VPN/TLS, and Kubernetes-based fault tolerance. IoMT devices offload tasks to MEC servers; SDN centrally manages networking/security; Kubernetes replicates and recovers MEC services automatically.

Title: A decentralised architecture for secure exchange of assets in data spaces: The case of SEDIMARK
Title_1: A decentralised architecture for secure exchange of assets in data spaces: The case of SEDIMARK
Authors: Erika Duriakova, Diarmuid O’Reilly-Morgan, Maroua Bahri, Maxime Costalonga, Gabriel Danciu, Septimiu Nechifor, Stefan-Cristian Jarcau, Tarek Elsaleh, Peipei Wu, Franck Le Gall, Grigorios Koutantos, Panagiotis Vlacheas, Luis Sánchez, Juan Ramón Santana, Pablo Sotres, Elias Tragos
Data movement: Metadata; Aggregates; Model parameters; Raw data
Responsibility: Published metadata, offers policies, (pre)processes data, sends results.
Responsibility_3: Finds suitable provider, requests data, receives data.
Architecture summary: SEDIMARK is a fully decentralised EU data-space marketplace using DLT, SSI, smart contracts, federated/distributed AI, and local data ownership. Providers keep data locally while enabling secure sharing, AI training, interoperability, and asset monetisation through distributed catalogues and policy-controlled exchange.

Title: Efficient Non-DHT-Based RC-Based Architecture for Fog Computing in Healthcare 4.0
Title_1: Efficient Non-DHT-Based RC-Based Architecture for Fog Computing in Healthcare 4.0
Authors: Indranil Roy, Reshmi Mitra, Nick Rahimi, Bidyut Gupta
Responsibility: Holds global resource table, also a group node.
Responsibility_3: Hold data, hold local resource table.
Responsibility_5: Group node, holds local resource table, communication goes via group head.
Responsibility_7: Connected to sensors, divides tasks over ring nodes.
Responsibility_9: Generate data, send to fog controller.
Architecture summary: A hierarchical RC-based non-DHT P2P fog-computing architecture for Healthcare 4.0 using publish/subscribe communication and resource-aware fog node grouping to reduce latency and improve scalable resource discovery.

Title: A multi-layered data service model for Cyber-Physical Production Networks
Title_1: A multi-layered data service model for Cyber-Physical Production Networks
Authors: Ada Bagozi, Devis Bianchini, Massimiliano Garda, Michele Melchiori, Anisa Rula
Coordination mechanism: Central orchestrator; Federated coordinator; Manual coordination
Responsibility: Holds data, receives request, executes computation.
Architecture summary: A three-layer service-oriented architecture for cyber-physical production networks that separates atomic services, factory-level composite services (CPPS), and cross-supply-chain services (CPPN). It supports modularity, interoperability, data sovereignty, RBAC-based governance, and both centralized and distributed deployments.

Title: Enabling secure and self-sovereign machine learning model exchange in manufacturing data spaces
Title_1: Enabling secure and self-sovereign machine learning model exchange in manufacturing data spaces
Authors: Tharindu Ranathunga, Alan McGibney, Sourabh Bharti
Responsibility: Hold data, train model locally, coordinate with other nodes.
Architecture summary: CORDS is a decentralized manufacturing data space framework enabling self-sovereign ML model sharing. Local data is semantically integrated via LDT; ML artefacts are advertised/shared through GDT using IDSA connectors, metadata brokers, contracts, and usage-control policies.

Title: PHIRI: Lessons for an extensive reuse of sensitive data in federated health research
Title_1: PHIRI: lessons for an extensive reuse of sensitive data in federated health research
Authors: Juan González-García, Javier Gonzalez-Galindo, Francisco Estupiñán-Romero; Martin Thißen, Ronan A. Lyons, Carlos Telleria-Orriols, Enrique Bernal-Delgado; on behalf of PHIRI
Security measures: Legal agreements; Authentication; Manual approval; Other: Container isolation
Responsibility: Defines research question
Responsibility_3: Builds pipeline/containers, aggregates results
Responsibility_5: Run analysis, return results.
Responsibility_7: Packages execution, run by participant nodes.
Architecture summary: PHIRI is a federated health research infrastructure based on the “data visiting” principle where data remains local and containerized analytical pipelines move to participant nodes. A coordination node orchestrates workflows, distributes Dockerized pipelines and CDMs, and aggregates local outputs from multiple institutions for joint analyses while preserving privacy and interoperability.

Title: Privacy-Preserving Hierarchical Federated Learning Over Data Spaces
Title_1: Privacy-Preserving Hierarchical Federated Learning over Data Spaces
Authors: Alexandros S. Kalafatelis, Vasileios Nikolakakis, Nikolaos Tsoulakos, Panagiotis Trakadas
Responsibility: Hold data, train local model on data.
Responsibility_3: Aggregate models from data space nodes, send to server.
Responsibility_5: Aggregate models from aggregator nodes.
Architecture summary: FLoDS is a hierarchical federated learning framework for Data Spaces where clients train locally, stewards enforce governance/privacy and aggregate updates securely, and a central orchestrator performs global fusion using encrypted steward updates and audit manifests.

Title: Devolution: A Symbiotic Cloud–Edge Framework for Real-Time Multimodal Retrieval in 6G-Based Surveillance IoT System
Title_1: DEVOLUTION: A Symbiotic Cloud–Edge Framework for Real-Time Multimodal Retrieval in 6G-Based Surveillance IoT System
Authors: Lingwu Meng, Guangchi Liu, Junzhou Luo
Responsibility: Collect data, pre-proccessing, send upward.
Responsibility_3: Store data locally, filter and send only the Top-K candidates up.
Responsibility_5: Compute
Architecture summary: DEVOLUTION is a cloud–edge collaborative multimodal retrieval framework for surveillance IoT. The cloud fine-tunes CN-CLIP models and manages hierarchical indexes, while edge nodes preprocess/store multimodal data and perform local retrieval using hierarchical clustering and BFBS beam search. Elasticsearch integrates distributed indexing and retrieval, with AES securing data transmission.

Title: Secure multi-party test case data generation through generative adversarial networks
Title_1: Secure Multi-party Test Case Data Generation Through Generative Adversarial Networks
Authors: Zheng Wang, Liutao Zhao, Fanyin Meng, Zhongshan Zhu, Yiqing Lu
Data output: Aggregates; Model parameters; Model; Synthetic data
Responsibility: Holds the local dataset, train on data.
Responsibility_3: Aggregate to a global model.
Architecture summary: FAT-CG is a federated GAN-based framework for generating syntactically valid industrial protocol test cases without sharing raw data. Participants locally preprocess and train models, exchange encrypted gradients/latent vectors with a central coordinator, and validate outputs through syntax and anomaly checking.

Title: From Technical Prerequisites to Improved Care: Distributed Edge AI for Tomographic Imaging
Title_1: From Technical Prerequisites to Improved Care: Distributed Edge AI for Tomographic Imaging
Authors: Bilgehan Akdemir, Hafiz Faheem Shahid, Mikael A. K. Brix, Juho Lääkkölä, Johirul Islam, Tanesh Kumar, Jarmo Reponen, Miika T. Nieminen, Erkki Harjula
Security measures: Legal agreements; Authentication; Isolated computation environment
Computation location: Cloud; Edge device; Central server; Federated databases
Responsibility: Generates data.
Responsibility_3: Processing, lightweight ML models.
Responsibility_5: processing, storage
Responsibility_7: Radiologist access, pre-processing, request for processing.
Architecture summary: Distributed 3-tier edge-cloud architecture for CBCT reconstruction and medical imaging AI. Data is processed as close as possible to the source using local and edge computing, with cloud used for heavier tasks. Supports distributed AI, containerized services, and privacy-aware healthcare processing.

Title: Toward an Edge-Friendly Distributed Object Store for Serverless Functions
Title_1: Toward an Edge-Friendly Distributed Object Store for Serverless Functions
Authors: Xin Chen, Manoj Prabhakar Paidiparthy, Liting Hu
Responsibility: Holds routing table to find other functions, offers function and necessary data for execution. Executes functions with container and returns results.
Architecture summary: Capybara is a decentralized edge-friendly distributed object store for serverless functions. It uses a DHT-based P2P overlay for scalable routing/storage and programmable handlers for customizable state management policies while co-locating data and computation on edge nodes.

Title: EuProGigant: A decentralized Federated Learning Approach based on Compute-to-Data and Gaia-X
Title_1: EuProGigant: A decentralized Federated Learning Approach based on Compute-to-Data and Gaia-X
Authors: Roman Gehrer, Stefan Dumss, Fabian Gast, Willi Wunschel, Frederic Schwill, Mateo Soša, Shiyang Zhou, Gerald H. Ristow, Tatevik Gharagyozyan, Clemens Heistracher, Manfred Grafinger, Matthias Weigold
Security measures: Legal agreements; Authentication; Isolated computation environment
Architecture summary: A decentralized federated learning architecture for manufacturing where data owners keep machine data locally on edge devices while model owners distribute training containers via Ocean Protocol Compute-to-Data. Gaia-X trust services and smart contracts coordinate trusted access, orchestration, and aggregation without a centralized authority.

Title: A Secure and Trustworthy Biometric Data Ecosystem for Cross-border Suspect Identification
Title_1: A Secure and Trustworthy Biometric Data Ecosystem for Cross-border Suspect Identification
Authors: Katerina Kyriakou, Apostolos Apostolaras, Polychronis Velentzas, Georgios Benos, Konstantinos Koutsoukos, Chrysostomos Symvoulidis, Kaitai Liang, Zeshun Shi, Asterios Leonidis, Kyriaki Miniadou, Eleni Veroni, Spyridon Evangelatos, Georgios Th. Papadopoulos, Thanasis Korakis
Data movement: Metadata; Aggregates; Encrypted data; Raw data
Responsibility: Stores data, offers results along policy. Request results from other nodes
Architecture summary: A decentralized biometric data-sharing ecosystem for cross-border suspect identification where LEAs keep local control of biometric databases while secure IDS connectors, blockchain smart contracts, and PETs (deep hashing, homomorphic encryption, AES/fuzzy extractors) enable encrypted distributed biometric search and controlled profile exchange.

Title: Connecting Manufacturing Sector Data Ecosystems with Federated Learning
Title_1: Connecting Manufacturing Sector Data Ecosystems with Federated Learning
Authors: Jonas Kallisch, Jorge Marx Gómez
Responsibility: Train local model, share with orchestrator through data space.
Responsibility_3: Request trained model from local node, using services or otherwise, aggregate results.
Architecture summary: The paper proposes integrating FL into manufacturing data ecosystems such as GAIA-X and EuProGiant. Data remain local at companies while FL clients train models collaboratively through federated coordination and catalog/discovery services. Architectures aim to preserve sovereignty while enabling cross-company analytics.

Title: Hydra Radio Access Network (Hydra-RAN): Multi-Functional Communications and Sensing Networks: A Hierarchical Framework for Task Distribution
Title_1: Hydra Radio Access Network (Hydra-RAN): Multi-Functional Communications and Sensing Networks: A Hierarchical Framework for Task Distribution
Authors: Rafid I. Abd, Kwang Soon Kim, Masoud Ardakani
Data movement: Aggregates; Model; Encrypted data; Raw data
Responsibility: Generate stream data.
Responsibility_3: Sensing and classification of environment, pre-processing of sensor data, execution of lightweight ML models.
Responsibility_5: Aggregation of edge results, execution of agents for real-time decision-making, coordination of scheduling.
Responsibility_7: Long-term learning model updates, management of historical data.
Architecture summary: Hydra-RAN is a hierarchical edge–fog–cloud framework for dynamic, context-aware task distribution in multifunctional communication and sensing networks. Lightweight low-latency tasks run at the edge, DRL-based scheduling occurs at the fog layer, and global orchestration/model training occurs in the cloud.

Title: Advancing Privacy-Preserving Health Care Analytics and Implementation of the Personal Health Train: Federated Deep Learning Study
Title_1: Advancing Privacy-Preserving Health Care Analytics and Implementation of the Personal Health Train: Federated Deep Learning Study
Authors: Ananya Choudhury; Leroy Volmer; Frank Martin; Rianne Fijten; Leonard Wee; Andre Dekker; Johan van Soest
Responsibility: Task orchestration, client handling.
Responsibility_3: Model aggregation.
Responsibility_5: Local training of model, export of parameters.
Architecture summary: PHT/Vantage6-based federated deep learning infrastructure connecting distributed hospital data stations through secure encrypted tracks, coordinated by a central server and secure aggregation server. Dockerized CNN models train locally at hospitals, only model weights are exchanged and aggregated centrally without sharing raw patient data.

Title: ATD Learning: A secure, smart, and decentralised learning method for big data environments
Title_1: ATD Learning: A secure, smart, and decentralized learning method for big data environments
Authors:
Laith Alzubaidi, Sabah Abdulazeez Jebur, Tanya Abdulsattar Jaber, Mohanad A. Mohammed, Haider A. Alwzwazy, Ahmed Saihood, Harshala Gammulle,
Jose Santamaria, Ye Duan, Clinton Fookes, Raja Jurdak, Yuantong Gu
Responsibility: Trains model on local data, combines with other models, communicates with other nodes. Logging on blockchain.
Architecture summary: Decentralized AI-to-data framework where models move to local data sources, nodes exchange encrypted features through blockchain-secured peer-to-peer coordination, and global learning is achieved via feature fusion and consensus validation.

Title: A Modular Architecture for Inter-Fog Environments
Title_1: A Modular Architecture for Inter-Fog Environments
Authors: Henrique Buzin, Alysson Oliveira, Mauro Marques, Patricia Della Mea Plentz
Responsibility: Generate data, send upward.
Responsibility_3: Orchestrates distribution of tasks between fog notes.
Responsibility_5: Compute, send result upward.
Responsibility_7: Combine results from fog nodes, send to cloud.
Architecture summary: Modular fog computing architecture enabling direct inter-fog communication, protocol abstraction (HTTP/CoAP), load balancing, configurable services, and cloud integration for smart-city IoT processing.

Title: A Reference Architecture for Enabling Interoperability and Data Sovereignty in the Agricultural Data Space
Title_1: A Reference Architecture for Enabling Interoperability and Data Sovereignty in the Agricultural Data Space
Authors: Rodrigo Falcão, Raghad Matar, Bernd Rauch, Frank Elberzhager, Matthias Koch
Responsibility: Hosts twin hub instance, stores data, allows connections and processes when necessary.
Responsibility_3: Upload data to twin hub node, approve usages. Request data / results from other farmers.
Responsibility_5: Request data / results from other farmers.
Architecture summary: Multi-instance “Twin-Hub” platform for agricultural data sharing. Farmers host digital field twins, manage fine-grained consent, and monitor access logs. Services access field data through standardized APIs or mediated exchange mechanisms to support interoperability and data sovereignty.

Title: Personal Health Train Architecture with Dynamic Cloud Staging
Title_1: Personal Health Train Architecture with Dynamic Cloud Staging
Authors: Luiz Olavo Bonino da Silva Santos, Luís Ferreira Pires1, Virginia Graciano Martinez, João Luiz Rebelo Moreira, Renata Silva Souza Guizzardi
Execution type: Query-based; Containerized execution; Other: API, Script, Message
Security measures: VPN; Authentication; Manual approval; Other: Encryption
Responsibility: Hosts data, executes trains, enforces access control.
Responsibility_3: Mediates communication with Data Controllers
Responsibility_5: Metadata indexing and discovery of stations/data.
Architecture summary: The architecture extends the Personal Health Train (PHT) paradigm, where algorithms ("trains") travel to data rather than moving data to analysts. Data remain under control of local Data Stations. When a station lacks computational resources, a cloud-based Staging Station is dynamically deployed in AWS. Required data are copied to this controlled cloud environment, containerized analysis is executed, results are returned to the original Data Station, and outputs may be reviewed before release. The architecture combines federated governance with elastic cloud computation while preserving local control and privacy compliance.

Title: Improving edge AI for industrial IoT applications with distributed learning using consensus
Title_1: Improving edge AI for industrial IoT applications with distributed learning using consensus
Authors: Samuel Fidelis, Márcio Castro, Frank Siqueira
Responsibility: Generate data, send upward.
Responsibility_3: Pre=processing, tinyML, send results upward.
Responsibility_5: Federated learning, send results upward.
Architecture summary: Distributed Edge AI architecture for IIoT where lightweight ML models run on mist/fog edge nodes, predictions are combined using a peer-to-peer consensus algorithm, and cloud resources are mainly used for summarized storage and dashboards.

Title: Complex Event Processing on the Edge - Bringing Data Consolidation and Processing closer to Wireless Sensor Networks
Title_1: Complex Event Processing on the Edge - Bringing Data Consolidation and Processing closer to Wireless Sensor Networks
Authors: David Merkl, Henry-Norbert Cocos
Data storage: Cloud; Edge device; Central server, database
Responsibility: Generate data, send upward.
Responsibility_3: Receive data from sensor nodes, coordinate computation.
Responsibility_5: Compute, send results upward.
Architecture summary: A ZigBee-based wireless sensor network sends environmental sensor data to a Raspberry Pi edge node running Siddhi CEP and MongoDB. The edge node preprocesses, filters, aggregates, and stores data locally before forwarding selected data to fog/cloud services for further analytics.

Title: Secure and Policy-Compliant Query Processing on Heterogeneous Computational Storage Architectures
Title_1: Secure and Policy-Compliant Query Processing on Heterogeneous Computational Storage Architectures
Authors: Harshavardhan Unnibhavi, David Cerdeira, Antonio Barbalace, Nuno Santos, Pramod Bhatotia
Security measures: Legal agreements; Authentication; Isolated computation environment
Responsibility: Sends data to main infrastructure, specifies access policies.
Responsibility_3: Requests results in the form of queries.
Responsibility_5: Stores data, processes, manages access based on policies.
Architecture summary: IronSafe is a secure, policy-compliant query processing system for heterogeneous computational storage architectures. Queries are partitioned between trusted x86 host and ARM storage nodes using SGX and TrustZone TEEs, while a trusted monitor performs attestation, policy enforcement, logging, and secure key management.

Title: Towards a Reference Enterprise Architecture to enforce Digital Sovereignty in International Data Spaces
Title_1: Towards a Reference Enterprise Architecture to enforce Digital Sovereignty in International Data Spaces
Authors: Danniar Reza Firdausy, Patrício de Alencar Silva, Maria-Eugenia Iacob, Marten van Sinderen
Component: Broker service provider + certification body + clearing house
Responsibility: Central device that allows nodes to find each other and connect.
Responsibility_3: Has data, shares metadata and decides usage policies. Optionally executes analysis/aggregation/cleaning through apps.
Responsibility_5: Finds the right data owner node using broker, accepts policy, receives results.
Architecture summary: Reference enterprise architecture for International Data Spaces (IDS) enabling sovereign cross-organizational data sharing through certified IDS connectors, broker services, usage policies, metadata publication, and reusable data apps while maintaining interoperability.

Title: SyncMesh: Improving Data Locality for Function-As-A-Service in Meshed Edge Networks
Title_1: SyncMesh: Improving Data Locality for Function-as-a-Service in Meshed Edge Networks
Authors: Daniel Habenicht, Kevin Kreutz, Soeren Becker, Jonathan Bader, Lauritz Thamsen, Odej Kao
Responsibility: Receives function calls, computes result from data, returns results.
Architecture summary: Decentralized mesh of autonomous edge nodes using OpenFaaS and containers. Each node stores local sensor data, processes it locally through transformer functions, and exchanges data with neighboring nodes on-demand to improve locality and reduce network traffic.

Title: KRAKEN: A Privacy-Preserving Data Market for Authentic Data
Title_1: KRAKEN: A Privacy-Preserving Data Market for Authentic Da
Authors: Karl Koch, Stephan Krenn, Tilen Marc, Stefan More, Sebastian Ramacher
Responsibility: Collect data, register with broker and send to cloud.
Responsibility_3: Holds data.
Responsibility_5: Handles connections with providers, computation nodes and consumers.
Responsibility_7: Fetch data from cloud, compute, send to consumer.
Responsibility_9: Request data with broker, receive from computation node.
Architecture summary: Privacy-preserving data marketplace using MPC and cryptographic protocols. Data owners keep encrypted/shared data in chosen cloud storage, define usage policies, and distributed computation nodes execute approved analytics or secure data sharing coordinated by a broker without exposing plaintext data to the broker.

Title: DataSHIELD: Taking the analysis to the data, not the data to the analysis
Title_1: DataSHIELD: taking the analysis to the data, not the data to the analysis
Authors:
Amadou Gaye, Yannick Marcon, Julia Isaeva, Philippe LaFlamme,
Andrew Turner, Elinor M Jones, Joel Minion, Andrew W Boyd,
Christopher J Newby, Marja-Liisa Nuotio, Rebecca Wilson,
Oliver Butters, Barnaby Murtagh, Ipek Demir, Dany Doiron,
Lisette Giepmans, Susan E Wallace, Isabelle Budin-Ljøsne,
Carsten Oliver Schmidt, Paolo Boffetta, Mathieu Boniol,
Maria Bota, Kim W Carter, Nick deKlerk, Chris Dibben,
Richard W Francis, Tero Hiekkalinna, Kristian Hveem,
Kirsti Kvaløy, Sean Millar, Ivan J Perry, Annette Peters,
Catherine M Phillips, Frank Popham, Gillian Raab, Eva Reischl,
Nuala Sheehan, Melanie Waldenberger, Markus Perola,
Edwin van den Heuvel, John Macleod, Bartha M Knoppers,
Ronald P Stolk,10, Isabel Fortier, Jennifer R Harris,
Bruce HR Woffenbuttel, Madeleine J Murtagh,
Vincent Ferretti and Paul R Burton
Execution type: Query-based; Other: Distributed/federated statistical computation (not machine-learning federated learning)
Security measures:
Firewall; Authentication; Manual approval; Other: Disclosure control rules (cell suppression, minimum counts)
Command whitelisting / approved functions only
Audit logging of commands
Responsibility: Sends analysis commands, coordinates execution, combines summaries, computes final results.
Responsibility_3: Communicates with AC and executes approved computations on local data.
Responsibility_5: Stores harmonized study data and exposes them for DataSHIELD.analysis
Responsibility_7: Stores institution data, unharmonized study data.
Architecture summary: DataSHIELD uses a federated architecture where a central Analysis Computer sends approved analysis commands to multiple Data Computers. Each Data Computer hosts an Opal database and server-side R environment behind a firewall. Computation occurs locally on each site's data. Only non-disclosive summary statistics (e.g., score vectors, information matrices, contingency tables) are returned to the Analysis Computer, which combines them to produce global results. Raw individual-level data never leave their originating institution.

Title: OSSDIP: Open Source Secure Data Infrastructure and Processes Supporting Data Visiting
Title_1: OSSDIP: Open Source Secure Data Infrastructure and Processes Supporting Data Visiting
Authors: Martin Weise, Filip Kovacevic, Nikolas Popper, Andreas Rauber
Execution type: Trusted environment; Other: VM-based isolated execution
Security measures: Differential privacy; VPN; Legal agreements; Firewall; Authentication; Manual approval
Data movement: Metadata; Aggregates; Encrypted data; Raw data
Responsibility: Central storage that holds sensitive data, isolated. Only accessible by administrator.
Responsibility_3: Access point for data owner, for ingress or egress of data.
Responsibility_5: Environment for analysis of data
Responsibility_7: Access point for data users, connects to Analyst-VM, ensures logging.
Architecture summary: OSSDIP is an open-source secure data visiting infrastructure that keeps sensitive data inside a protected central Data Node while allowing analysts to work on isolated temporary VMs via remote desktop access. The system combines network segmentation, VM isolation, firewall barriers, monitoring, legal agreements, approval workflows, and auditing to prevent unauthorized data leakage while still enabling exploratory analysis.

Title: Gaia: Geo-distributed machine learning approaching LAN speeds
Title_1: Gaia: Geo-Distributed Machine Learning Approaching LAN Speeds
Authors: Kevin Hsieh, Aaron Harlap, Nandita Vijaykumar, Dimitris Konomis, Gregory R. Ganger, Phillip B. Gibbons, Onur Mutlu
Responsibility: Uses data to create local model.
Responsibility_3: Combines models of workers within data center, exchanges with parameter servers of other data centers.
Architecture summary: Gaia is a geo-distributed ML system built on a parameter-server architecture. Each datacenter maintains a local approximate copy of the global model. Local workers synchronize through BSP/SSP inside datacenters, while datacenters synchronize over WANs using Approximate Synchronous Parallel (ASP), which filters insignificant updates to reduce WAN communication while preserving convergence guarantees.

Title: Jarvis: Large-scale Server Monitoring with Adaptive Near-data Processing
Title_1: Jarvis: Large-scale Server Monitoring with Adaptive Near-data Processing
Authors: Atul Sandur, ChanHo Park, Stavros Volos, Gul Agha, Myeongjae Jeon
Responsibility: Sends query down, aggregates results to compute the final query output.
Responsibility_3: Sends query down, aggregates results, sends results up.
Responsibility_5: Holds data, answers queries by processing and sending results up.
Architecture summary: Jarvis is a decentralized near-data stream analytics system for datacenter monitoring. Queries are partitioned dynamically between data source nodes and stream processors using adaptive data-level partitioning. Local runtimes adjust operator load factors to reduce network transfer and improve throughput under changing resource conditions.

Title: The Blue Dataverse: A System for Marine Data Sovereignty
Title_1: The Blue Dataverse: A System for Marine Data Sovereignty
Authors: Vasileios Karagiannis, Mays AL-Naday, Tom De Block
Data movement: Metadata; Aggregates; Model parameters; Model; Raw data
Responsibility: Host data, offer with policies.
Responsibility_3: Provides computation according to policies.
Architecture summary: The Blue Dataverse is a federated marine data space where providers expose sovereign datasets through connectors enforcing usage policies. Federated services manage identity, metadata discovery, and semantic interoperability. App providers discover datasets, agree to policies, and receive data directly from providers for IoT analytics.

Title: The Challenges and Prerequisites of Data Stream Processing in Fog Environment for Digital Twin in Smart Industry
Title_1: The Challenges and Prerequisites of Data Stream Processing in Fog Environment for Digital Twin in Smart Industry
Authors: Ameer B. A. Alaasam
Responsibility: Generate data streams from industrial systems
Responsibility_3: Local preprocessing, low-latency analytics, stream processing
Responsibility_5: Long-term processing, storage, advanced analytics
Architecture summary: A hierarchical fog-cloud Digital Twin architecture where loosely coupled containerized processing services operate across edge/fog/cloud layers. Data layers and processing layers are separated to support portability, stateful stream processing, replication, low latency, and distributed analytics for industrial IoT systems.

Title: Shc: Distributed query processing for non-relational data store
Title_1: SHC: Distributed Query Processing for Non-Relational Data Store
Authors: Weiqing Yang, Mingjie Tang, Yongyang Yu, Yanbo Liang, Bikas Saha
Responsibility: Receives incoming queries and distributes them over the nodes below.
Responsibility_3: Holds data. Retrieves data from storage, and worker computes.
Architecture summary: SHC is a Spark SQL–HBase connector enabling distributed relational query processing over non-relational HBase storage. It extends Spark SQL with HBase-aware optimization (partition pruning, predicate pushdown, locality optimization), supports multiple encodings, and includes credential/token management for secure multi-cluster access.
